nebula Academy
Module · Practice

Applied
Tarot

Everything you've learned — the arcana, the spreads, the positions — now becomes a real reading. Five complete topic frameworks, five worked examples you can use immediately.

In this module

Module Overview

1 How to create a complete reading ↓
2 Love & Relationships ↓
3 Ex or a New Person ↓
4 Career & Money ↓
5 Purpose & Karma ↓
6 Final Tips ↓

How to create
a complete
reading

Section 01 · Foundation

From request to reading

A client never arrives with a tarot question. They arrive with a situation. Your first job — before you touch a single card — is to listen and translate it into a reading structure that can actually answer it.

"I don't know what's happening with my relationship." "I'm stuck at work and don't know what to do." "I feel lost and don't know which direction to take."

None of these are questions the cards can answer directly. They're states of confusion, emotional overwhelm, a sense of being stuck. Your job is to translate them into a reading structure.

The five layers

A complete reading
follows a natural arc

1
Foundation — What is the core energy right now? Before anything else, establish context. What is actually happening? This is your anchor for everything that follows.
2
Inner world — What is the person carrying beneath the surface? What the client says and what they're actually experiencing are often two different things. This is where the real story usually lives.
3
External — What is happening around them? Other people, circumstances, forces they may not be seeing clearly. The situation doesn't exist in a vacuum.
4
Block or challenge — What is actually getting in the way? Not just "things are hard," but specifically what is preventing movement.
5
Direction — Where is this heading, or what is the best next step? Every reading should end here. Your client came for a reason. Give them somewhere to go.
The rules

Four principles worth keeping close

Start broad, then go deep

Always establish context before diving into specifics. If you jump straight to "what does my partner feel" without knowing what the relationship energy is right now, you're reading in the dark.

One question, one position

Never ask two things in one card. "What does my partner feel and what will they do next?" — that's two separate questions. The card can only hold one. Split them.

Each question earns its place

If you removed a question and the reading would still make complete sense without it — that question doesn't belong. Every position should add something the others can't.

The last question gives direction

Your client came to you because they're stuck. Don't leave them with a description of where they are. Give them somewhere to go.

Love &
Relationships

Section 02 · Spread

5 positions · 1 full picture

A five-position spread that gives you a complete picture of a relationship from the inside out. It starts not with feelings — but with the deeper question of why these two people met. Click each position to explore what it reveals.

✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
✺
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Karmic task

The lesson both people are here to learn through each other

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThe "why" beneath the relationship — not what the couple wants from each other, but what they're here to learn. This card often reveals patterns or wounds that neither person is consciously aware of yet.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🌊
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Feelings

What the person is actually experiencing emotionally

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThe emotional body — what the person is actually experiencing. When Feelings and Thoughts don't align, that gap is one of the most important pieces of information in the reading. Name it.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
💭
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Thoughts

The mental layer — beliefs and internal narratives at play

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsHow the person is making sense of what they feel. It can confirm the emotion, contradict it, or complicate it. Often this is where the real conflict lives.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
⚡
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Intentions

What the person is actually moving toward — their will

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsNot what the person feels, not what they think — but what they are quietly deciding. What action are they preparing to take, even if they haven't said it yet?
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
✨
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Development

The likely trajectory if current energies continue

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsWhere this is heading if things continue as they are. Frame it honestly: "This is where things are heading right now." That framing gives the client agency — the future isn't sealed, it's shaped by what happens next.
Use when:

A client wants to understand another person's inner world — and the deeper reason this connection exists at all.

  • "I really like them but I can't read them at all.
  • "I love them but something feels off.
  • "I don't know if we have a future together.
What makes this spread different is where it starts. Most clients ask about feelings. This spread asks something deeper first: why did these two people meet at all?
Section 02 · Example

Love & Relationships
reading example

Example

A client asks: "I've been seeing someone for three months. I really like them but I can't read them at all. What's actually going on?"

Karmic task
The Hierophant
tap to reveal
A lesson around commitment and structure.These two met to work through something around what a "real" relationship is supposed to look like — what each person believes a partnership should be. That question will come up between them.
Feelings
Knight of Cups
tap to reveal
Deep romantic feeling — more than they show.This person is emotionally engaged — genuinely, perhaps more than they're showing. The Knight of Cups doesn't do half-measures. But Knights can also retreat when they feel vulnerable.
Thoughts
Seven of Swords
tap to reveal
Strategic, cautious — processing privately.Something is being held back, or thought through before it gets expressed. This isn't deception — it's someone processing internally before they show their hand.
Intentions
Two of Wands
tap to reveal
Looking ahead — weighing whether to invest fully.This person is actively thinking about the future and whether to invest more fully in this connection. They haven't committed yet. But they're standing at a threshold, deciding.
Development
The Lovers
tap to reveal
A defining choice is coming.What's coming is a moment of real choice — not a slow drift, but a defining point where this relationship becomes something clear, or doesn't.
Reading synthesis

There's more here than the surface suggests. The person you can't read is actually deeply feeling — the Knight of Cups doesn't lie about that. But feeling and showing aren't the same thing. The mind is working privately, carefully, thinking through something it isn't ready to speak yet. This isn't coldness. It's someone who feels a lot and moves deliberately. What's coming is a turning point — a moment where both of you will need to decide what this actually is. And underneath all of it, there's a question neither of you may have asked out loud yet: what does a real relationship actually look like to each of you? That question is the thread running through this whole connection. The answer to it will shape everything.

Ex or a
New Person

Section 03 · Spread 1 — Ex

The Ex Spread

For the client who's still emotionally in it — who needs to know what's actually real, not just what they're hoping for. Click each position to explore what it reveals.

✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
👤
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Them

Their emotional reality right now — not their behavior

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThis card is about emotional reality — not behavior. Someone may not text or reach out, yet the card shows deep feeling. That's the gap between what someone feels and what they show. Read the feeling beneath the behavior.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🪞
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
You

The client's specific state in this dynamic right now

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsNot who the client is as a person — but the specific state they're in right now, in relation to this particular situation. Someone generally confident might pull a card of withdrawal here. That's how they're showing up in this dynamic.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🔗
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Connection

What's energetically alive between these two people

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThis is the most important card in the spread. A strong connection card matters even when surrounding cards look difficult. The Lovers here when both people seem stuck doesn't mean you got it wrong — it means the bond is real, just blocked.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🚧
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Obstacle

What's in the way — always read in context of card 3

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThe same card reads differently in a warm versus cold spread. In a warm spread, an obstacle card like Eight of Swords is fear. In a cold one, it's avoidance. Always read the obstacle in relation to what you found in the connection position.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🧭
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Direction

Where this is heading if nothing changes

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsFrame this carefully. It's not a verdict — it's a trajectory. The energy is moving this way right now. If something shifts in either person, the direction shifts too.
Use when:

The client still has emotional skin in the game. Not curiosity — genuine investment. They need to know what's actually real.

  • "Will my ex and I get back together?
  • "Does my ex still have feelings for me?
  • "Why did they suddenly go silent?
Example

A client asks: "Will my ex and I get back together?"

Them
The Star
tap to reveal
Quiet hope — still present, still real.Whatever happened between them, this person hasn't emotionally closed the door. They may have gone silent. But The Star says: the feeling is still there, underneath the silence.
You
Four of Cups
tap to reveal
Withdrawn, waiting for a signal.The client feels a lot but is holding back, waiting for a sign before they move. They're asking "will we get back together?" — but what they're really saying is: "is it safe to try?"
Connection
The Lovers
tap to reveal
A real bond — it didn't disappear.This isn't just chemistry — it's alignment. Whatever happened, it didn't erase what was there. The connection is still energetically alive. And yet nothing is moving — card four tells you why.
Obstacle
Eight of Swords
tap to reveal
Fear — self-constructed limitation.The barrier isn't external. It's the story each person tells themselves about what will happen if they reach out. Both people are waiting. Both people are afraid. And so nothing moves.
Direction
Ace of Cups
tap to reveal
A new emotional beginning — if someone moves.Not a reunion on its own, but the possibility of one. The energy is there. The opening is there. What the Ace of Cups requires is someone to move toward it.
Reading synthesis

The feelings didn't go away on either side — they just went quiet. Both people are caught in the same pattern: waiting for the other to make it safe first. The connection is real, the bond is still there, but fear is running the show right now. The direction isn't closed — it's waiting. What shifts this isn't time. It's one honest moment.

Section 03 · Spread 2 — New Person

The New Person Spread

For the client at the beginning of something — when the question is still "is this worth paying attention to?" Click each position to explore what it reveals.

✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
✺
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Why you met

The soul-level purpose of this encounter

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsNot what the client wants from this person, but what's underneath the meeting at a deeper level — what pattern or growth edge is being activated here. Often the most surprising card in the spread.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
💞
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Feelings

What's actually moving energetically between them

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsNot what's been said or shown — what's actually moving energetically. Pay attention when this card and Intentions don't match. Feelings and intentions don't always point in the same direction. When they don't, name it.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
⚡
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Intentions

What this person is actually moving toward

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsNot what they feel — what they're actually moving toward. What are they preparing to do, or quietly deciding? This card often tells the client something they couldn't have known just from their experience of the connection.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🔭
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Where it's heading

The quality of what's building — momentum or surface?

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsAvoid specific predictions ("in three months you'll be together"). Instead speak to quality: Is this connection gaining momentum or staying surface-level? Is it moving toward depth or staying casual?
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
💡
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Advice

What the client needs to keep in mind as this unfolds

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThe closing anchor. Should feel like something the client can actually take away — not a vague note, but something practical. How should they show up? What do they need to stay aware of?
Example

A client asks: "Is this person the right one for me?"

Why you met
Wheel of Fortune
tap to reveal
This meeting wasn't random — it's a turning point.Something larger is at work here. This person is arriving at a specific moment in the client's life for a reason. The timing itself is information.
Feelings
Two of Cups
tap to reveal
Genuine mutual resonance — it goes both ways.This isn't one-sided interest or wishful thinking — the emotional current is moving in both directions. What's present here is real.
Intentions
Knight of Pentacles
tap to reveal
Steady, serious — not here for something casual.The Knight of Pentacles moves slowly but commits fully. They're not impulsive. They're genuinely considering whether to invest — and take that decision seriously.
Where heading
Ten of Pentacles
tap to reveal
Long-term potential — something with real roots.This card points toward stability, structure, something that could genuinely build into a life. One of the strongest cards you can pull in this position.
Advice
The High Priestess
tap to reveal
Don't rush — trust the pace of this.The High Priestess asks the client to trust the pace of this connection rather than push for certainty before it's ready. The answers are coming — but this one unfolds on its own timeline.
Reading synthesis

This connection has the feel of something that arrived on purpose. The energy between them is mutual and grounded — not a flash of chemistry, but something quieter and more substantial. Their intentions are genuine. The direction points toward something lasting. The only thing the client needs to resist is the urge to know everything now. This one unfolds slowly — and that's exactly why it's worth paying attention to.

Career &
Money

Section 04 · Spread

5 positions · 1 full picture

Not just a snapshot of the problem — a map that includes the resource, the obstacle, and the way through. Click each position to explore what it reveals.

✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
⚖️
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Where you stand

Current energy — not achievement, but what the client is carrying

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsEnergy, not achievement. A difficult card here isn't saying the client is failing — it's naming what they're carrying right now. Read it without softening, but also without dramatizing. This card sets context for everything else.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
✦
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
What's working

What's already moving in the right direction — often overlooked

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThe card clients most often overlook when they come in feeling stuck. Something is always moving in the right direction. Your job is to find it and name it clearly. This card gives the client something solid to stand on.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🚧
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Block

What's preventing movement — read in context of card 1

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsRead in context of card one. If the client is in a strong position, the block is often subtle — a mindset, a hesitation. If it's a difficult season, the block may be structural. Always frame it as something that can be moved through — not a wall, a door that needs a key.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🌱
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Opportunity

Present or incoming — check if it's active or still building

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsRequires timing awareness. Some opportunities are already present and being missed. Others are incoming — not fully arrived yet. Look at whether the card feels active and immediate, or more potential and building.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🧭
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Direction

Where the current energy is heading if nothing changes

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsA trajectory, not a verdict. "This is what the cards see moving toward right now." That's the most honest framing — and delivered clearly, it's enough.
Use when:

A client feels stuck, uncertain, or at a crossroads — and can't see the way forward clearly.

  • "I've been in the same job for years and nothing's moving.
  • "I keep working hard but can't seem to get ahead financially.
  • "I'm about to make a big decision and need clarity on the energy around it.
Example

A client asks: "I've been in the same job for three years and nothing's moving. Is this going anywhere?"

Where you stand
Five of Pentacles
tap to reveal
Depleted — but not seeing what's available.The two figures in the Five of Pentacles are walking past a lit window. The resource is there. They just aren't looking at it. This card isn't just confirming the struggle — it's asking: what are you not seeing that's already available to you?
What's working
Ace of Wands
tap to reveal
Pure creative fire — the spark before the flame.Right next to a card of depletion sits raw creative drive — the impulse to build something new. Nothing has shifted in the outside world yet. But this energy is real, it's the client's, and it's the most important resource in the spread.
Block
The Moon
tap to reveal
Something unacknowledged — operating below the surface.The block isn't a bad boss or a slow market. It's something internal and unexamined — a fear that's affecting decisions without the client being fully aware of it. That means the obstacle isn't outside their control. It just needs to be brought into the light.
Opportunity
Seven of Cups
tap to reveal
Multiple options — but not all are real.The card isn't saying there's no opportunity — it's saying the client may be overwhelmed by options, or drawn to what looks exciting rather than what's actually right. What do you actually want, versus what just looks appealing?
Direction
The World
tap to reveal
Completion — the destination is solid.After the depletion, the fog, the scattered options — The World says: the path leads somewhere real. But The World doesn't skip the work. It comes after the full cycle completes — which means every step between here and there matters.
Reading synthesis

The stuck feeling is real — but it's not the whole picture. There's genuine creative energy here that hasn't found its channel yet, and a destination that's actually solid. What's in the way isn't a lack of potential. It's an unexamined fear and a tendency to look at too many options without committing to one. The moment this client gets honest about what's actually holding them back — and chooses one clear direction over the noise of possibilities — the energy has somewhere to go. The World doesn't show up for people who aren't moving toward something real.

Purpose &
Karma

Section 05 · Spread

5 positions · 1 full picture

For the client asking bigger questions — not what will happen, but why things keep happening the way they do. Click each position to explore what it reveals.

✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🌀
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Soul's lesson

The core theme of this chapter of life

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThe lens for everything else — the overarching theme of this chapter. Read it first, name it clearly, and let it frame the whole spread. It's not a problem to solve. It's the territory this person is moving through.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
⚓
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
What you carry

What arrived before this chapter — weight and resource both

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThis position holds two things at once: weight and resource. A difficult card names a karmic burden — something still unresolved. A strong card names a gift that was earned through past experience or something deeper. Either way, this gives language to something the client has been carrying without a name for it.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🔥
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
What's asked

Where life is applying pressure right now

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsThe card of active growth — the uncomfortable territory, the place where life is applying pressure. Frame it carefully: not as something being done to the client, but as an invitation. This is where the work is. And the work is the point.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🎁
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
Your gift

What they naturally bring that has value beyond themselves

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsOne of the most important cards to read well — clients almost never trust their own strengths without outside confirmation. Don't be vague. Go specific: not "you're intuitive," but what kind of intuition, expressed how, in service of what. This card is permission.
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
🌿
✦ ✦ ✦✦ ✦ ✦
The path

The direction that aligns with who the client actually is

↑ tap to explore

What this card revealsRead as orientation, not destination. Not "this is what you're supposed to become" — but "this is the energy that aligns with who you actually are." Purpose in this spread isn't a job title or a life plan. It's a direction. A way of moving through the world that feels true.
Use when:

A client is asking bigger questions — not what will happen, but why things keep happening the way they do.

  • "I keep ending up in the same situations no matter what I do.
  • "I feel like there's something I'm supposed to be doing but I can't figure out what it is.
  • "Nothing I build feels like enough.
This spread is for the client who needs to understand not just where they're going, but what they're here to do.
Example

A client asks: "I keep feeling like I'm supposed to be doing something more meaningful — but I don't know what it is, or if I'm just making it up."

Soul's lesson
Judgement
tap to reveal
A deep reckoning — who am I beneath everything I was told to be?This client is in the middle of shedding an old identity. There's a call they've been hearing — maybe for years — and this card says the time to answer it has arrived. Not someday. Now.
What you carry
Ten of Swords
tap to reveal
A formative wound — but the sky is already lightening.Something collapsed completely. A loss or betrayal that felt final. But the darkest point has already passed. What this client carries isn't just the pain — it's the fact that they survived it. And everything that survival quietly taught them.
What's asked
Strength
tap to reveal
Stay present and open — not force, inner work.The Strength card doesn't ask for pushing harder. It asks for the ability to stay present when everything is pushing toward hardening or shutting down. Meeting the situation from the inside, not managing it from the outside.
Your gift
The Hierophant
tap to reveal
The ability to transmit earned wisdom others can receive.This person carries real wisdom — not theoretical, but earned — and has a natural ability to pass it on. People come to this client because something about their presence says: I know the way through this. That's the foundation of a purpose.
The path
Six of Wands
tap to reveal
Visible leadership — stop holding it quietly.The path forward runs through being seen. Not performance, not ego — stepping into a role where others can actually see and follow. This client's purpose isn't realized in private. It requires presence.
Reading synthesis

This isn't someone making things up. The longing for meaning is real, and the cards confirm it. There's a significant awakening happening — the kind that asks you to release an old version of yourself and step into something truer. The wound from the past isn't incidental to the purpose: it's part of it. What this person has survived is exactly what gives them the capacity to guide others. The gift is earned, the wisdom is real — and now the path is asking for something uncomfortable: to stop holding it quietly and start leading with it. The Six of Wands doesn't appear for people who have nothing worth following. It appears for people who are finally ready to go first.

Final Tips

Section 06 · Synthesis

How to synthesize

This is where most readings either land or fall apart. The most common mistake is reading a spread as a list. A reading moves. It has a through-line. Each card connects to the next — and finding that connection is your job.

Read as a story, not a list

Start with the card that sets the context — usually the first position. Then let the others respond to it. What does the second card say in light of the first? What does the third add, change, or complicate?

And look for the tension. The most interesting moment in any spread is usually between two cards that seem to pull in opposite directions — the connection and the obstacle, the gift and the wound, the opportunity and the block. That gap is almost always where the real message lives. Don't smooth it over. Name it. That's where the reading gets honest.

One clear takeaway

Before you close any reading — ask yourself one question: what is the one thing this client needs to hear?

Not five things. One. Everything else in the reading supports that central message. The other cards provide context, nuance, texture. But there's always one thing at the center. If you can't name it, the synthesis isn't finished yet.

Section 06 · Boundaries

Hold your ground

When you sit with someone in the middle of something painful, there's a pull to do more than read — to reassure, to promise, to take on some of the weight. Resist that pull. Not because you don't care. But because it isn't your weight to carry.

The distinction that matters: you read the energy. You don't carry the outcome. The cards show what's present. What the client does with that is entirely theirs. Your responsibility ends at clarity.

When a client pushes for certainty — "just tell me yes or no" — they're asking you to hold something that isn't yours to hold. The most honest framing is always directional: this is where the energy is moving right now. That's what tarot offers. And delivered clearly, it's enough.

Four situations

Tricky cases

↗

Client pushes for certainty

Reframe the direction, not the outcome. "The energy is moving toward X" is more honest than a yes or no — and actually more useful, because it gives the client something to work with rather than something to wait for.

⇄

Spread is unclear or contradictory

Don't force coherence that isn't there. Name the tension directly. A contradictory spread usually means the situation itself is unresolved — and that's real information. The cards are mirroring what's actually happening.

◈

Difficult card, sensitive client

Lead with context before content. What position is the card in? What does the surrounding spread say? Give the card meaning before you give it a name. The frame matters as much as the card.

✓

Client already knows the answer

Don't over-explain. Sometimes the reading confirms what someone already feels but hasn't let themselves say out loud. Name it simply. Give it space. Let it land. The reading did its job.

"A good reading doesn't take away the mystery. It gives you a way to stand inside it."

— Mary K. Greer, Tarot teacher & author

The work

Not to predict. Not to rescue. Not to take the uncertainty away. But to bring a little more light to something that felt unclear — and to give the client something solid to stand on while they figure out what to do next.

You've covered the full arc of this block. The archetypes, the spreads, the structure of a reading, the moments that require skill and the moments that require courage. You have the tools. The rest comes with practice.

Nebula Academy  ·  Applied Tarot
